title: Checking and managing file sharing permissions
what_it_is: File sharing permissions are the settings that control who can see, open or change a file you've shared — for example a Word document, a OneDrive folder or a file posted in a Teams channel.
why_use_it: Checking permissions before you share, and reviewing them afterwards, means the right people can get to a file — and no one else can.
how_to: Before sharing a file, right-click it in OneDrive or Teams and select Share.
In the Share window, select the current access level shown — it usually says something like "People in [organisation] with the link" or "Specific people".
Choose who the file is for. "Specific people" limits access to named individuals — use this for anything personal or 1:1.
Choose the access level for those people: Can view lets someone open and read the file only. Can edit lets them open the file and change it.
Select Apply, then select Send or Copy link to share.
To check or change permissions on a file that's already shared, right-click the file, select Share, then select Manage access.
The Manage access screen lists everyone who currently has access, and whether each person can view or edit.
To remove someone's access, select their name, then select Remove or Stop sharing.
Once you've removed access, that person's link will no longer open the file.
